summary:
To measure the hardness of Co-Cr alloys, the Rockwell hardness test is employed due to their high modulus of elasticity and durability requirements. This non-destructive test effectively evaluates such highly resilient metals commonly found in demanding engineering applications.
To summarize Agent2’s reasoning:

The Rockwell hardness test is used to measure the hardness of Co-Cr alloys because they have high elastic moduli and require durable properties; thus, this test accurately assesses their resilience suitable for various engineering uses. [1 sentence] 

[Note: Adjusted from two into one concise sentence while retaining core information.]
